# GitHub Discussions — Proposed Categories

GitHub Discussions can't be defined from a file (they're configured in the repo UI),
so this document is the **proposed setup** for maintainers to create under
**Settings → Discussions**.

| Category | Format | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| 📣 **Announcements** | Announcement | Releases, roadmap updates, calls for contributors. Maintainers post. |
| 💬 **General** | Open | Anything that doesn't fit elsewhere. |
| 💡 **Pattern Ideas** | Open | Float a pattern before opening a formal "New Pattern" issue; refine scope and naming. |
| 🧭 **Architecture Q&A** | Question / Answer | "RAG vs fine-tuning?", "which retrieval strategy?" — real decisions, answerable by patterns. |
| 🗺️ **Roadmap & Prioritization** | Open | Debate what to build next and how to phase it. |
| 🏗️ **Project & Process** | Open | Structure, conventions, tooling, website, governance. |
| 🙌 **Show & Tell** | Open | Systems you built using MAP patterns; case studies. |

## Guidelines to post in each category description

- Be specific and cite sources where you can.
- Architecture Q&A answers should link to the relevant MAP pattern(s) — if a good
  answer has no pattern to link, that's a signal to open a **New Pattern** issue.
- Keep it welcoming and framework-neutral.

## Relationship to issues

- **Discussions** = open-ended: ideas, questions, prioritization.
- **Issues** = actionable, tracked work (a pattern to write, a bug to fix).

When a discussion converges on concrete work, open the matching issue and link back.
